research_confidence.notes: Architecture and content types well-documented via KA engineering blog and open-source repos (Perseus, Wonder Blocks). Exact accessibility tree roles (AXButton vs AXLink) for specific buttons are inferred from standard React/WCAG patterns since direct macOS accessibility tree dumps were not available. Button text ('Check', 'Skip', 'Next question') confirmed from KA support articles and community posts. Timing values are conservative estimates based on SPA behavior with GraphQL backend and Fastly CDN. Perseus widget types confirmed from GitHub source (30+ widget dirs). Khanmigo AI tutor may introduce unexpected modals on paid/district accounts.

timing.notes: React SPA with GraphQL Federation backend (Go services + Python monolith) on Google Cloud via Fastly CDN. Page transitions are client-side route changes (no full reload) but involve GraphQL data fetching. Exercise question transitions within a set are faster (~1.5s) since they swap content in-place via Perseus. Video pages load YouTube iframe which adds latency. Initial page loads slower than in-page transitions. Wonder Blocks design system components used throughout. Conservative estimates account for slow network and dynamic content hydration.

Course-wide navigation arrow (~23x25px AXButton at top of page). Skips past the entire current unit. Never the right target for lesson-by-lesson progression. Use 'Up next: <title>' AXLink for post-video advancement; use sidebar lesson links for within-unit nav.
Course-wide navigation arrow that skips backward past completed content. Never the right target for forward progression.
accessibility skip-link, a no-op for navigation — observed wasted cycle 2026-06-11 (LLM picked it as the nav answer)
accessibility skip-link, a no-op for navigation — observed wasted cycle 2026-06-11 (LLM picked it as the nav answer)

platform_quirks.description: Khanmigo AI tutor (GPT-4 based) may appear as a floating button or modal dialog on exercise pages for paid/district accounts ($9/month or district-provided). It presents as a chat overlay that can obscure exercise content. If detected, it should be dismissed (close button, typically an X in top-right of overlay) before interacting with the exercise. The Khanmigo icon is usually in the bottom-right corner.

After completing a practice exercise with exactly 1 incorrect answer, a 'Bonus question' button may appear. If offered, the bonus question MUST be either answered or skipped — leaving it unanswered means the exercise is NOT marked complete and no credit is given. Answering correctly upgrades score to Proficient. Some exercises (reading comprehension, teacher-locked, small question banks) do not offer bonus questions.

The 'Skip' button on exercises automatically marks the question as INCORRECT. It also shows a step-by-step solution/hint walkthrough. The old separate 'Hint' button has been replaced by this Skip flow. Do NOT click Skip unless the system is stuck or unable to answer. The Skip button grays out after a question is successfully answered.

Videos must be watched in their entirety (every second) to be marked as complete. Skipping even a few seconds results in an incomplete status. Playback speed up to 2x is allowed and still counts for completion. For teacher-assigned videos, 90% must be watched at no greater than 2x speed. The video must play to the very end — closing early even with seconds remaining marks incomplete.

Under heavy traffic or network restrictions (e.g., YouTube blocked), Khan Academy may serve a fallback video player instead of the YouTube embed. This player lacks speed controls and subtitle customization. A 'Switch to full player' link appears at bottom-right to return to YouTube player.

Mastery Challenges (math courses only) have a 12-hour window to complete once started, and only one can be taken per 12-hour period. They consist of exactly 6 questions covering 3 skills (2 questions each). They appear on the main Course page only when specific conditions are met (Familiar on 3+ skills, Proficient on 1+ skill). Questions are randomized so 2 questions per skill may not be consecutive.

Math expression inputs require specific formatting. Parentheses must be used explicitly (e.g., '1/(2p)' not '1/2p'). Square brackets shown in hints should be entered as parentheses. The expression evaluator follows strict PEMDAS order of operations. Formatting errors cause correct answers to be marked wrong.

First-time or returning users may see a 'Welcome to Khan Academy!' modal asking 'So we can give you the right tools, let us know if you're a... Learner / Teacher / Parent'. This must be dismissed before content is accessible. District admin goal-setting popups may also appear on login. Cookie consent banner may appear on first visit.

The left sidebar navigation panel opens by default but can be collapsed by clicking a left-pointing arrow button. If collapsed, lesson activity items are not visible in the accessibility tree for navigation. The sidebar must be expanded (click right-pointing arrow) to navigate between activities within a lesson.

Quizzes, Unit Tests, and Course Challenges are sequential — once you advance past a question, you cannot go back to it. The 'Skip' button marks it as incorrect permanently for that attempt. Course Challenges contain 30 questions. Unit Tests cover all unit skills. Mastery Challenges have 6 questions.

Khan Academy exercises use the Perseus system with 30+ widget types including: radio (multiple choice), input-number, numeric-input, expression (math), interactive-graphs (Mafs library, rebuilt for a11y), categorizer, sorter, orderer, dropdown, matcher, graded-group, graded-group-set, label-image, table, passage, plotter, free-response, matrix, number-line, definition, explanation, image, measurer, and more. Each widget type has different interaction patterns. Some (interactive-graph, sorter, orderer, matcher) require drag-and-drop or complex mouse interactions that may need screenshot-based LLM guidance.

On unit/course navigation pages, clicking video or article items may open content in a modal overlay rather than navigating to a new page. The modal has the video's title as a hyperlink at the top — clicking the title navigates to the full lesson page. This modal behavior is inconsistent and may differ between content types.

UNIVERSAL RULE: Before calling send_to_llm to choose an answer, ALL hidden content must be extracted into the prompt context. For dropdowns/comboboxes, this means: open EACH popup, enumerate its menu items via discover_menu (role=AXMenuItem), capture the option list, close the popup, then call send_to_llm with the full options on every popup. The LLM must NEVER be asked to guess options from screenshot+context alone — it gets the literal AX-rendered option strings so its returned answer matches what the click handler will see. Same principle applies to any hidden/collapsed UI: modals, expandable sections, tooltips with answer content, etc.

platform_quirks.quirk: Cmd+R (page reload) CLEARS staged answers on the current question (per Jesse 2026-06-11 — legitimate clearing mechanism). NEVER reload between clicking Check and confirming the submission registered: a reload in that window can eat the submission (suspected cause of the Q4 'X' on the 2026-06-11 unit test — the stale BT pressed Cmd+R 3s after Check).

completion_model.video: Must watch every second of the video (no skipping). Playback up to 2x is fine. Completion shown as full colored bar with checkmark icon in sidebar. For assignments: 90% watched at ≤2x speed.
completion_model.article: Auto-completed when visited and scrolled through. No explicit 'Mark as complete' button. Simply opening and scrolling to the bottom registers as read. Green checkmark appears in sidebar.
completion_model.exercise: Must answer all questions in the set (typically 4-7 questions). >70% correct = 'Practiced' level. All correct = 'Proficient'. With bonus question: 1 wrong + bonus correct = 'Proficient'. Exercise NOT complete until all questions (including bonus if offered) are answered or skipped.
completion_model.quiz: Must answer all questions. Scored as percentage. Results affect mastery levels for skills covered. No going back to previous questions.
completion_model.unit_test: Must answer all questions. Results can level skills up or down. Covers all skills in the unit. No going back.
completion_model.course_challenge: 30 questions covering entire course. Results affect mastery levels across all course skills. No going back.
completion_model.mastery_challenge: 6 questions covering 3 skills (2 each). Math courses only. 12-hour window. 2 correct on a skill = level up, 2 wrong = level down, 1 each = no change.

bot_detection.known_measures: Khan Academy uses Fastly CDN which provides basic bot detection and rate limiting. No CAPTCHA documented for normal browsing. The platform tracks video watching behavior closely (scrub detection, play state monitoring via YouTube API) to prevent credit gaming. Google Cloud App Engine has default DDoS protections. Static assets served from kastatic.org and kasandbox.org domains. No explicit Cloudflare or dedicated bot management layer documented in public sources.
bot_detection.recommended_delays: Add 1-2s random delay between actions. For exercises, wait at least 2s after Check before clicking Next. For video pages, do not interact with the player programmatically faster than a human would. Avoid submitting answers faster than 5s per question to prevent pattern detection. Between page navigations, wait 2-3s minimum. For rapid sequential exercises, vary timing between 3-8s per question.

EXERCISE.description: Exercise screens present a question (rendered by the Perseus system) with various interactive widgets for answering. The question appears in the main content area. At the bottom-right are 'Check' and 'Skip' buttons. A progress indicator (dots or fraction like '3 of 7') shows position in the question set. After checking, correct answers show green with a ding sound; incorrect answers show red. Left sidebar shows current position within the lesson. Exercise types include practice (4-7 questions), quizzes, unit tests, course challenges (30 questions), and mastery challenges (6 questions).

subtypes.name: multiple_choice
subtypes.description: Radio button selection from 4-5 answer choices. Most common exercise type (~74% of exercises produce number or MC response). Each choice is a clickable radio button with text/math label.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click radio button for desired answer -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.note: CHECK-ENABLE RACE + NO-TOGGLE (observed live 2026-06-11, Q5): after clicking checkbox/radio choices, Check enables only after a React re-render — clicking Check immediately can no-op silently (BT 'succeeded', nothing submitted). ALWAYS wait >= 1.5s between the last choice click and the Check click. AND: checkboxes TOGGLE — before clicking any choice, read the SCREENSHOT for already-selected boxes; if the intended answers are already selected, click Check ONLY. Re-clicking a selected box deselects it and submits a wrong/partial answer.

subtypes.prompt_block:
=== DETECTED: RADIO BUTTONS (multiple-choice exercise) ===

Your tree has AXRadioButton elements. This is a multiple-choice exercise screen.

COMPLETE BT PATTERN:
{
  "type": "sequence",
  "children": [
    {
      "type": "action",
      "action": "extract_question",
      "params": {
        "question": {"role": "AXStaticText", "parent_contains": "Exercise:"},
        "options": {"role": "AXRadioButton"}
      },
      "store": "q"
    },
    {
      "type": "action",
      "action": "send_to_llm",
      "params": {
        "question": "$q.question_text",
        "question_type": "solve_choice",
        "options": "$q.options",
        "context": "$q.reference_texts"
      },
      "store": "llm"
    },
    {
      "type": "action",
      "action": "find_and_click",
      "params": {
        "target": "$llm.answer",
        "role": "AXRadioButton",
        "strategy": "focus_space",
        "match_mode": "exact"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "action",
      "action": "find_and_click",
      "params": {
        "target": "Check",
        "role": "AXButton",
        "strategy": "mouse_click",
        "post_delay": 2.0,
        "match_mode": "exact"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "action",
      "action": "store_qa",
      "params": {
        "question": "$q.question_text",
        "answer": "$llm.answer",
        "question_type": "solve_choice"
      }
    }
  ]
}

HOW IT WORKS:
1. extract_question scopes to AXWebArea, uses your params to find question text
   and radio button options. You MUST set parent_contains to the actual container
   name from the tree where the question lives.
   Returns: {question_text: str, options: [str], reference_texts: [str]}
2. send_to_llm calls Spark /api/v1/generate which routes to Gemini 2.5 Pro.
   Maps options to A/B/C letters, asks LLM for letter, maps back to exact text.
   Returns: {success: true, answer: "exact option text from the list"}
3. find_and_click finds the radio button whose name contains $llm.answer.
   Strategy focus_space: focuses element then presses Space (required for
   browser radio buttons — mouse_click doesn't reliably toggle radio state).
4. find_and_click("Check") submits the answer. post_delay 2.0 gives the SPA
   time to process and load the next question.
5. store_qa saves the Q&A pair to Mac-side SQLite for knowledge building.

EXPECTED_NEXT:
- Same screen type (next question in the exercise)
- TRANSITION (score card or "Next" button after all questions answered)
- NAVIGATION (if exercise completes back to content list)
Do NOT include the current screen in expected_next (creates infinite loops).

WRONG ANSWER DETECTION:
If the Mac reports the same skeleton hash after executing this BT, it means
the same question was re-presented — the answer was wrong. This triggers
reconsultation with failure_reason="wrong_answer_same_question".

VARIANT: If the tree also has AXTextArea (radio + text field combo):
Use question_type="solve_choice" with has_text_field=True in params.
LLM returns {answer: "option text", text_response: "reflection text"}.
Add find_and_type for the text field AFTER clicking the radio button.

VARIANT: If question has images/diagrams visible in screenshot:
Use question_type="solve_complex" instead. This sends the screenshot to
Gemini 2.5 Pro for multimodal analysis.

SUBMIT BUTTON NAMES (varies by platform):
- Khan Academy: "Check" (AXButton)
- Coursera: "Submit" (AXButton)
- Acellus: varies — look at the screenshot

subtypes.name: multiple_select
subtypes.description: Checkbox selection where multiple answers may be correct. Instructions say 'Select all that apply' or 'Choose all answers that apply'.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click one or more checkboxes -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.note: CHECK-ENABLE RACE + NO-TOGGLE (observed live 2026-06-11, Q5): after clicking checkbox/radio choices, Check enables only after a React re-render — clicking Check immediately can no-op silently (BT 'succeeded', nothing submitted). ALWAYS wait >= 1.5s between the last choice click and the Check click. AND: checkboxes TOGGLE — before clicking any choice, read the SCREENSHOT for already-selected boxes; if the intended answers are already selected, click Check ONLY. Re-clicking a selected box deselects it and submits a wrong/partial answer.

subtypes.name: numeric_input
subtypes.description: Text field expecting a numeric answer. May have math keypad overlay. Field accepts typed numbers. Perseus widgets: input-number, numeric-input.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click text field -> type numeric answer -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.rule: CLASSIFY BY THE ANSWER WIDGET, NOT THE IMAGE. If the content area has an AXTextField answer box (HAS_TEXT_INPUT), this is a NUMERIC/TEXT-INPUT question — even when an image/graph is present and even when chrome AXComboBox/AXPopUpButton (Chrome tab strip / Share / Extensions) are in the tree. Do NOT classify as dropdown/matcher/assessment and do NOT use solve_assessment_page or find_all(AXComboBox) — those FAIL here (observed repeatedly on Quiz 1 KE questions: DROPDOWN_FILL_BLANK / DROPDOWN_KE_GRAPHS / solve_assessment_page all returned NONE / never typed the answer).
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: TEXT-INPUT solve (the ONLY correct pattern when an AXTextField answer box exists): 1) extract_question {question:{role:AXStaticText,min_length:25}, text:[{role:AXStaticText,min_length:10}]}. 2) send_to_llm question_type=solve (full screenshot auto-attached -> the LLM reads the image/graph as CONTEXT and computes the numeric answer; e.g. KE scales with mass at equal speed: 10x mass -> 10x KE). 3) find_and_type {target:"", role:AXTextField, text:.answer} (target="" = first content field; the unit e.g. GJ/J is fixed beside it). 4) wait_for_element Check -> find_and_click Check post_delay=2.5. 5) store_qa. The image is QUESTION CONTEXT, never a separate widget to actuate.
operational_notes.note: Skeleton-hash collision: multiple distinct numeric quiz questions (refueling-aircraft KE, daughter KE, KE-graph) share hash 40f3b3e6 — the worker MUST re-read the current question from screenshot each rebuild; never assume a fixed answer.

subtypes.name: expression_input
subtypes.description: Math expression field accepting algebraic expressions, fractions, exponents. Uses specialized math input with LaTeX rendering. Requires precise formatting (parentheses matter, PEMDAS enforced). Perseus widget: expression.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click expression field -> type math expression (use parentheses carefully) -> click 'Check'

subtypes.name: dropdown
subtypes.description: One or more dropdown menus embedded in a sentence or passage. User selects the correct option from each dropdown. Perseus widget: dropdown.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: click dropdown -> select option from popup list -> repeat for all dropdowns -> click 'Check'

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY inline dropdown(s)/combobox(es) on Khan Perseus exercises
operational_notes.rule: ENUMERATE OPTIONS BEFORE ASK. Verified BT engine primitives only.
operational_notes.note: Khan AXComboBox dropdowns have placeholder name='Select an answer' until clicked, and options are NOT in the AX tree until the popup opens. Universal pattern: open each popup, enumerate options via discover_menu, close, store options on the popup item via store_to_current; THEN call send_to_llm with all popups (each carrying real options); THEN iterate to select. Lookup keys by $popup.description (NOT $popup.label — placeholder collides across popups). find_and_click accepts element=$ref from a for_each-bound popup, no separate selector needed.
verified_bt_engine_primitives: for_each items=$list variable=popup do={...} — binds current item as $popup
store: 'key' on action — writes return to blackboard[key]
store_to_current: 'field' inside for_each — writes return to current popup dict
find_and_click with element=$popup.element — accepts pre-resolved element ref
discover_menu role=AXMenuItem returns list of option strings
select_dropdown_option owns its own open/strategy ladder/normalize/verify

params.question: Fill in each dropdown with the correct option based on the screenshot and the surrounding question context. Each item's `label` is its position; each item's `options` lists the actual menu choices available for THAT dropdown. Return the EXACT option string from options[] for each item.

known_failure_modes: Element staleness: if Wonder Blocks re-renders popups after one opens, $popup.element from outer find_all may go stale on iter 2+. If that happens (only first dropdown opens, rest fail with element-not-found), the workaround would be sequential unrolled blocks (find_all + index access) per popup — but try the canonical for_each version first.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: CANONICAL BT (handler param names verified):

ccm_observed_failures.16:54_observation: find_and_click element=$popup.element → resolves target='' role=None → ACTION RETURNED NONE
ccm_observed_failures.root_cause: find_and_click handler does NOT read 'element' param. Only 'click'/'click_element' do.
ccm_observed_failures.fix: Use 'click' action with element=$popup (whole dict, not just .element)
ccm_observed_failures.17:00_observation: click element=$popup → re-find by description='Select an answer' (placeholder) fails because handle_click hardcodes role='AXPopUpButton', but Khan uses AXComboBox. Re-find returns None.
ccm_observed_failures.17:00_fix: Pass element=$popup.element (raw AX ref, not dict) — handler skips re-find entirely and uses the ref directly.
ccm_observed_failures.17:10_observation: All 3 popups successfully enumerated (click + discover_menu + escape worked end-to-end). send_to_llm crashed: claude_cli exit 1 with empty stderr, /api/v1/generate returned 500. API log showed question='' — likely Claude CLI needs a non-empty question.
ccm_observed_failures.17:10_fix: Add 'question' field to send_to_llm.params in the canonical BT template.
ccm_observed_failures.17:15_observation: Enumeration phase fully working (all 3 popups opened/enumerated/closed). send_to_llm returned matches but with only 2 unique keys for 3 popups (matches_keys=['Select an answer', 'The forces act on'] — both colliding). Lookup_match for popup_1 picked the value keyed under 'The forces act on' which was actually the answer for a DIFFERENT popup, so click on this popup's menu (options=['the same not selected', 'different']) failed to find that text.
ccm_observed_failures.17:15_fix:
1. parse_matching_response now also keys matches[str(idx)] = best.
2. Worker generates UNROLLED selection phase with literal '0','1','2' keys.
ccm_observed_failures.17:22_observation: Enumeration phase ran, but item[0].options=[] in api log — popup 0's discover_menu found 0 AXMenuItem because menu hadn't rendered yet (0.7s post_delay too short on first popup open). LLM got empty options for item 1, returned empty answer, matches['0']='', lookup_match returned empty, select_dropdown_option failed with 'missing option' param.
ccm_observed_failures.17:22_fix: Bump click post_delay 0.7→1.2s + insert wait_for_element AXMenuItem timeout=2.0s between click and discover_menu so discover_menu only fires once a menu is actually rendered.
ccm_observed_failures.17:25_observation: Enumeration phase fully worked (all 3 popups, wait_for_element added). discover_menu returned options like ['the same not', 'different'] — the 'the same' option's AX name 'the same not selected' got 9 chars stripped by Mac's _extract_menu_items (which strips ' selected'), leaving 'the same not'. LLM picked 'the same not' (the only thing on offer). select_dropdown_option opened the popup and normalized menu items to ['the same', 'different'] — 'the same not' was no match → click failed.
ccm_observed_failures.17:25_fix: Server-side workaround in _solve_matching_with_gemini: strip dangling ' not' suffix from options before passing to LLM (undoes Mac's overzealous strip). Real fix would be Mac-side: _extract_menu_items should strip ' not selected' (13 chars) before falling back to ' selected' (9 chars). Mac is frozen, so server workaround is the pragmatic path.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus table-with-dropdowns (e.g. kinetic-energy 'Determines KE?' rows: mass/volume/height/speed)
operational_notes.rule: DEDUPE POPUPS + DISAMBIGUATE ROWS. Khan double-wraps each visible AXComboBox AND Mac's `label` heuristic returns the column header for every popup, NOT the row label.
operational_notes.note: OBSERVED 2026-05-19: find_all role=AXComboBox returned 8 entries for 4 visible rows. Visible_bbox values (e.g. y=546, 596, 646, 696) repeat exactly — pairs are SEQUENTIAL by index: popups[0..3] are rows top-to-bottom (first AX pass), popups[4..7] are the same rows again (wrapper pass). NOT interleaved (don't take every-other). Additionally, EVERY popup's `label` came back as the column header text (e.g. 'mass' for all 4 rows of the kinetic-energy table) because Mac's _find_preceding_label walks back to the nearest AXStaticText above the popup — which is the column header, not the per-row quantity label. With 8 popups all labeled 'mass', the LLM has no row context in the items list and must guess from the screenshot alone — observed result: 2 of 4 rows wrong, submitted, 'Not quite yet... Try again' state.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: 1) After find_all role=AXComboBox -> $popups, iterate $popups for discover_menu (all 8 — opening/closing wrappers doesn't change form state). 2) send_to_llm with $popups (all 8) but in the `question` text explicitly tell the model: 'Items[0..N/2-1] are the visible dropdowns in TOP-TO-BOTTOM row order. The remaining items are wrapper duplicates — produce ONE answer per visible row only, indexed 0 through N/2-1.' Include the row labels VERBATIM in the question text (e.g. 'Row 0=mass, Row 1=volume, Row 2=height, Row 3=speed') — derived from the screenshot, since item.label is unreliable here. 3) Use lookup_match key='0' / '1' / '2' / '3' (UNROLLED, only first half of popups) → select_dropdown_option trigger_element=$popups.0.element / $popups.1.element / $popups.2.element / $popups.3.element. DO NOT call select_dropdown_option on popups.4-7 — wrapper writes can overwrite or toggle the first pass.
operational_notes.wrong_answer_recovery: If 'Try again' AXButton + 'Not quite yet...' AXStaticText are visible, click 'Try again' first (preserves prior fills), then redo the deduped 4 selects with corrected answers. Same recovery pattern as note 2 (partial-submission).

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus inline-fill-blank dropdowns (e.g. 'Kinetic energy and X have a ___ relationship.' style — dropdowns embedded WITHIN running sentences, NOT in a table with column labels)
operational_notes.rule: INLINE FILL-BLANK PATTERN: dropdowns sit inside running sentences; the `label` heuristic returns garbage (truncated sentence fragments or column headers from unrelated structures). Sort popups by (visible_bbox.y, visible_bbox.x) and reconstruct sentence context FROM THE AX TREE — don't rely on item.label.
operational_notes.note: OBSERVED 2026-05-19 on Khan ms-physics kinetic-energy lesson 2/4: question is 'Kinetic energy and speed have a __ relationship. If an object's speed doubles while its mass stays the same, its kinetic energy __.' AX tree has 4 visible AXComboBox arranged in 2x2 grid (two sentences, two blanks each). visible_bbox: (955,380), (1155,420), (963,482), (1199,522). Each blank is preceded AND followed by AXStaticText fragments — the fragments BEFORE the popup (e.g. 'Kinetic energy and speed have a') and AFTER (e.g. 'relationship.') together form the sentence context for that blank. Mac's _find_preceding_label picks only the immediate preceding AXStaticText which may be a punctuation '.' from the previous sentence — useless. Solution: in the worker BT, do NOT pass $popups.label to the LLM. Instead, embed the FULL sentence text VERBATIM from the screenshot in the send_to_llm question, with each blank annotated by index (e.g. 'Item 0 = first blank in sentence 1'). The LLM uses the screenshot to align positions.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: 1) find_all role=AXComboBox -> $popups. 2) for_each $popups: click + wait_for_element AXMenuItem + discover_menu + press_escape — populates options on each popup. 3) send_to_llm with $popups AND a question that QUOTES THE FULL SENTENCES verbatim from the screenshot and explicitly labels each blank: 'Blank 0 (first dropdown, position top-left): fills <sentence fragment>. Blank 1 (top-right): fills <fragment>. Blank 2 (middle-left): ...'. Don't ask the LLM to use item.label. 4) lookup_match key='0'..'3' UNROLLED + select_dropdown_option × N (one per visible popup — count from find_all, no wrapper dedupe needed for this pattern since the AX tree has exactly one AXComboBox per visible blank when bboxes are all distinct y-positions). 5) wait_for_element Check + find_and_click Check.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT use $q.label or $popup.label in send_to_llm — for this pattern those fields contain sentence-fragment garbage. The LLM must be given the FULL sentence context derived from the screenshot, not the heuristic label.

operational_notes.by: claude-primary (synthesized from Clarity Tier 2 Deep Research)
operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus dropdown widget (Wonder Blocks SingleSelect) — inline fill-blank AND table-row variants
operational_notes.rule: Wonder Blocks SingleSelect renders <button role=combobox> + <div role=listbox> + <div role=option>. Selection is React-synthetic-event-driven. DOM-click on opener + DOM-click on option (mouse_click strategy) is the ONLY working path. ax_press fails silently on Chrome. Keyboard Tab/Arrow/Enter is functionally equivalent to mouse_click (both route to same OptionItem.onClick). Existing select_dropdown_option with open_strategy='mouse_click' IS structurally correct.
bt_refinements: open_wait: 1.0s (was 0.7) — Perseus needs time to reconcile React state after select before next opener click

For inline-fill-blank: send FULL SENTENCE TEXT to send_to_llm per popup so LLM does blank→answer binding. Do NOT rely on item.label (Wonder Blocks SingleSelect doesn't set visibleLabel/ariaLabel on bare AXComboBox — Mac's _find_preceding_label returns ambient sentence fragments, not stable identifiers).
For table-style: position-based ordering (left-to-right, top-to-bottom by bbox) is the most reliable disambiguator.
operational_notes.discover_menu_role_fallback: Wonder Blocks renders <div role=option>. macOS may expose this as AXMenuItem OR AXOption depending on listbox context. If discover_menu(role='AXMenuItem') returns empty options list, RETRY with role='AXOption'. This is the MOST LIKELY silent failure point — a discover_menu that returns 0 options means subsequent select_dropdown_option will have no menu to choose from.
operational_notes.historical_context: Attempts 1-5 on this widget class collapsed on HTTP 422 from send_to_llm (oversized screenshot payload). Mac wire-format fix a3a3881 (2026-05-19 18:48 UTC) likely resolves this. Verify store_to_current accumulation inside for_each — confirm per-popup options list actually flows into send_to_llm's items.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan dropdown matching — lookup_match key format mismatch
operational_notes.rule: send_to_llm question_type=solve_matching returns matches dict keyed by FULL ROW TEXT (popup_desc/label), NOT by integer index. lookup_match with key='0' returns None when parse_matching_response couldn't parse the LLM response into numbered-line format. select_dropdown_option then errors with 'missing option param'.
operational_notes.observed: 2026-05-19 23:28: send_to_llm returned matches_keys=['The ball moved down and to the right, bounced off the ground and is in the air, moving to the...'] — single long-text key, no '0'/'1'/'2'/'3' keys. lookup_match key='0' returned None. select_dropdown_option failed: missing 'option'.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
INSTEAD of lookup_match key='0' followed by select_dropdown_option option=$chosen_0, use this safer pattern that handles BOTH key formats:
  Option A (preferred — direct mapping): for_each $matches.matches with variable=match: select_dropdown_option trigger_element=... option=$match (assuming the for_each binds (key, value) pairs).
  Option B (explicit-key per popup): determine each popup's expected key from $popups[i].popup_desc or $popups[i].label and use lookup_match key=$popups.0.popup_desc instead of key='0'. The label/popup_desc IS what parse_matching_response uses as the key.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT assume matches dict has integer-string keys '0'/'1'/'2'/'3' — parse_matching_response only adds those when the LLM response is in literal 'N: answer' line format. If the LLM returns JSON or row-labeled format, integer keys may be absent. Always use the popup's identifying field (popup_desc or label) as the lookup_match key to be safe.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus multi-image multi-system question (e.g. 'How did the PE of EACH system change?' with separate image pairs for distinct physical systems and one dropdown per system)
operational_notes.rule: PER-SYSTEM PER-REGION BT (Patch B from Clarity DR). When a question references MULTIPLE distinct sub-systems (gravity AND electric, ball AND spring, etc.) with separate image pairs, do NOT solve in one multi-blob send_to_llm call — restructure to per-system. Each system gets its own region screenshot + per-system vision call + per-system dropdown click. This avoids the cross-system context bleed where the LLM tries to reason about both systems at once and gets one or both wrong.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
PHASE 1 DISCOVER (multi-system case): 
  1a. extract_question — get question text + identify how many systems are referenced (count 'each system', count distinct AXImage pairs, count dropdowns).
  1b. find_all role=AXComboBox -> $popups. Number of popups = number of systems for these widgets.
  1c. For EACH popup: read AXFrame (visible_bbox), compute a screenshot_region = expanded rectangle covering the popup PLUS the image pair above it (typically ~200px above the popup, full width of question area).
  1d. Per-popup vision call: send_to_llm question_type=solve_complex with the REGION screenshot ONLY (or the full screenshot annotated with which region to focus). Prompt: 'Focus ONLY on the [Nth] system shown in this region. Describe the before/after state visible in the images. Apply physics rules to determine the correct answer for THIS system's dropdown only. Return JSON: {answer: "more|less|same"}.'
PHASE 2 SOLVE per system:
  2a. For popup i with answer from phase 1d: click $popups[i].element to open menu, wait_for_element AXMenuItem, find_and_click target=answer role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact, wait 0.5s.
PHASE 3 SUBMIT:
  3a. find_and_click 'Check' role=AXButton match_mode=exact post_delay=2.5.
PHASE 4 VERIFY:
  4a. If 'Try again' / 'Not quite' modal appears → wrong-answer-RCA universal rule fires (full stop, do NOT blind-retry).
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT use single solve_matching with all popups in one send_to_llm — the LLM context-blends across sub-systems and tends to get the directionality wrong on one system because it's anchored on the other. Per-system isolation is what Clarity DR confirmed works.

operational_notes.rule: USE THE UNROLLED PATTERN, NOT a for_each over the whole answer string. Observed failure (637b998b): worker classified as a CUSTOM label (EXERCISE_DROPDOWN_KE_GRAPHS) and ran for_each  with the comma-joined answer -> select_dropdown_option success=False. The canonical UNROLLED pattern (lookup_match key=0/1/2/3 -> select_dropdown_option trigger_element=.N.element option=) is the proven path. ALWAYS classify these as the canonical "dropdown" subtype so this pattern is used.
operational_notes.note: taeys-hands AT-SPI confirms: (1) select_dropdown_option ALREADY normalizes the " not selected"/" selected" aria suffix and verifies the AXComboBox value flips — pass the BARE option text per box (mass / speed), one per popup, NOT the joined string. (2) WRAPPER-DUP: if find_all returns 2x AXComboBox for the visible blanks, operate on the FIRST HALF only (top-to-bottom); wrapper writes overwrite the first pass. (3) VALIDATE per box: AXComboBox name flips from "Select an answer" to the chosen value before moving to the next. (4) Fallback if a click genuinely will not commit: keyboard-by-POSITION (focus opener, ArrowDown to the option index from discover_menu, Enter) — keyboard == mouse_click (same OptionItem.onClick), sidesteps text matching entirely.

subtypes.name: interactive_graph
subtypes.description: Graphing widget where users plot points, draw lines, or manipulate graph elements. Built with Mafs React library. Supports keyboard and screen reader. Perseus widget: interactive-graphs. Includes number-line widget.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: interact with graph (drag points, draw lines, use keyboard arrows) -> click 'Check'. Complex — likely needs screenshot + LLM visual analysis.

operational_notes.by: claude-primary (synthesized from Clarity Tier 2 Deep Research)
operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus interactive_graph (Mafs + @use-gesture/react) — graph-plot-point widget. Any screen with AXButtons named 'Point N at X comma Y' and instruction 'Drag the blue dot to plot points'.
operational_notes.rule: USE KEYBOARD NAV, NOT MOUSE DRAG. Khan's graph widget binds native PointerEvent listeners via @use-gesture/react. CGEventPost-based mouse events do NOT trigger the widget — they fall through to text-selection. The mouse drag handler is CONFIRMED 0% effective on this widget. Use the keyboard path the widget exposes for screen-readers.
operational_notes.diagnosis_root_cause: Perseus widget migrated to Mafs + @use-gesture/react with eventOptions:{passive:false} binding native PointerEvent listeners. The visible draggable circle (visiblePointRef) is aria-hidden=true — invisible to AX. The 'Point N at X comma Y' AXButton is the focusableHandle <g> element (tabIndex=0, role=button, no cx/cy → zero-size by design) — it's a keyboard target, not a mouse target. Mac's CGEventPost generates MouseEvents which the widget's PointerEvent listeners ignore.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
For each Point N to plot (target coords x_data, y_data):
  1) Read current position from AXButton.name pattern 'Point N at <cur_x> comma <cur_y>'.
  2) Compute delta = (x_data - cur_x, y_data - cur_y).
  3) Focus the handle: click_at(visible_point_pixel_center) — must hit the visiblePoint's 48px hitbox at the graph origin pixel for points still at (0,0), or its current rendered location. Use mouse_click strategy (NOT ax_press — that fails on aria-hidden parents). If click_at doesn't focus, fall back to press_key 'Tab' to walk to the right element.
  4) Verify focus: wait_for_element with AXFocused on focusableHandle, OR read AXButton name again to confirm it's the right Point N.
  5) press_key sequence: |delta_x| repetitions of ArrowRight (delta_x>0) or ArrowLeft (delta_x<0); then |delta_y| repetitions of ArrowUp (delta_y>0) or ArrowDown (delta_y<0). Default arrow step = 1/50 of graph span; with Shift = 1/10; with Alt/Meta = 1/200. For integer-grid exercises (like the kinetic-energy table where masses are 1,2,3,4 and KE is 2,4,6,8 at speed=2) the snap step is typically 1 unit per arrow if snapStep=1 in GraphConfigContext.
  6) wait_for_element with name pattern 'Point N at <target_x> comma <target_y>' (max_wait 3.0s) to verify the move registered. If not, the snap step may be smaller — try more arrows or use Shift+Arrow for larger jumps.
  7) Move to next Point.
  8) After all points placed: find_and_click target='Check' role=AXButton match_mode=exact.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use the `drag` handler with CGEventPost on this widget — 0% success rate, confirmed in Perseus source.
DO NOT use ax_press to focus — aria-hidden parents silently swallow it. Use mouse_click click_at on the visible point center.
DO NOT rely on the visible-blue-dot AX representation — it's aria-hidden=true, invisible to the tree.
open_questions_for_next_iteration: Snap-step value (snapStep from GraphConfigContext) per exercise — defaults assumed integer-grid but Clarity flagged variability. If integer-grid arrows don't land on target, try Shift+Arrow (1/10 span) or Alt+Arrow (1/200 span) to find the right increment.
Multi-point loops: verify each AXButton name update before proceeding to next point — Khan's React render may lag the AX update.
LimitedPointGraph vs UnlimitedPointGraph branch — Limited has pre-placed dots (the case observed); Unlimited requires click-to-add first.
operational_notes.fallback_if_keyboard_fails: Option B from Clarity DR: implement a CDP-injection handler that dispatches Input.dispatchMouseEvent with type='pointerDown' via Chrome DevTools Protocol. Requires new Mac handler. Don't attempt until keyboard path is empirically proven 0%.

operational_notes.screen_variant: EXERCISE_GRAPH_DRAG_PLOT/PARTIAL — anti-pattern observed 2026-05-19
operational_notes.rule: ONE-CLICK 'TRY AGAIN' IS NEVER A COMPLETE BT. Dismissing the modal returns you to the unsolved state — you still must plot the points.
operational_notes.note: OBSERVED 2026-05-19: worker generated a BT consisting solely of `find_and_click target='Try again'` for the partial-fill 'You're almost there' state on the kinetic-energy graph-plot exercise. Tree changed (modal dismissed) so Mac reported success=True, validator marked unvalidated→validated path. But the EXERCISE was not solved — the same screen with all 4 points still at (0,0) came back on the next encounter. The worker truncated its BT to the precondition (dismiss modal) without including the actual solve (keyboard-nav plot the 4 points).
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: When you see 'Try again' AXButton + 'Point N at 0 comma 0' buttons together: the BT MUST include BOTH the modal-dismiss AND the full keyboard-nav point-plot sequence. Concretely: (1) find_and_click 'Try again' to dismiss the modal, (2) wait 1.0s, (3) FOR EACH of the 4 Point buttons that are still 'at 0 comma 0' (read the AX name to confirm), click_at the visible point pixel center to focus the focusableHandle, (4) press_key sequence to step to the target (x,y) per the master interactive_graph operational_note above, (5) wait_for_element to verify the AX name updated to 'Point N at <target_x> comma <target_y>', (6) repeat for next point, (7) find_and_click 'Check'. NEVER end the BT after step 1.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: BT consisting ONLY of `find_and_click 'Try again'` is treated as success by Mac/validator (tree changed) but does NOT advance the exercise. Same screen recurs. Always include the full re-solve sequence after the modal dismiss.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan graph-plot keyboard-nav CALIBRATION — observed actual arrow step is NOT Clarity's documented 1/50 default
operational_notes.rule: READ AX NAME AFTER EACH ARROW PRESS TO CALIBRATE SNAP STEP. Don't assume 1 arrow = 1 unit or 1/50 of span. The actual snap step varies per exercise.
operational_notes.note: EMPIRICAL OBSERVATION 2026-05-19 on kinetic-energy graph (Point 1 starting at (0,0) for target (1,2)): worker pressed 1 Right + 2 Up arrows. AX result: 'Point 1 at 2.5 comma 10.' So this exercise's step is 2.5 x-units per Right and 5.0 y-units per Up — NOT the 1/50 default from Clarity's note. Each exercise has its own snapStep from GraphConfigContext. The arrow key DOES move the point (keyboard nav confirmed working), but the magnitude must be CALIBRATED per encounter. Also: Tab focus did NOT reliably advance past Point 2 (Points 3-4 stayed at (0,0)) — Tab may need an alternate strategy (click_at on next point's pixel, or press_key Tab multiple times).
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
Two-pass calibration approach:
  PASS 1 — CALIBRATE: focus Point 1 (click_at on its visible pixel center). Press one ArrowRight, then one ArrowUp. wait 0.5s. Re-read the AX tree (use find_all role=AXButton + filter to Point N names). Parse 'Point 1 at <dx> comma <dy>' — dx and dy are now the per-arrow step sizes for this exercise's graph. Store as $step_x and $step_y.
  PASS 2 — PLOT: for each Point N to plot at target (tx, ty): compute remaining_x = (tx - current_x) / step_x; remaining_y = (ty - current_y) / step_y. Round to integer arrow counts. Press ArrowRight (or Left) that many times; ArrowUp (or Down) that many times. wait_for_element 'Point N at <tx> comma <ty>' (max 3s).
  TO ADVANCE TO NEXT POINT: if Tab doesn't focus the next point (verify by reading focus after Tab), use click_at on the next Point's visible pixel center directly. The visible pixel is at: graph_origin_px_x + (current_x / x_axis_max) * graph_width_px, graph_origin_px_y - (current_y / y_axis_max) * graph_height_px (Y inverted because pixel-y grows downward). Read graph dimensions from the screenshot.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Don't hardcode arrow counts based on data-unit deltas. Don't trust Tab to advance focus through all 4 points without verification — confirm each focus via the AX name of the focused element before pressing more arrows.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan graph-plot — CRITICAL pixel-derivation + no-reset clarification
operational_notes.rule: TWO HARD CONSTRAINTS observed 2026-05-19: (1) Khan does NOT reset point positions between Check attempts — points stay where you last placed them; you must compute delta from CURRENT positions, not from (0,0). (2) The click_at to focus a Point MUST hit the visible blue dot's PIXEL POSITION in the screenshot — the AXButton bbox [711, 856, 0, 0] is an offscreen sentinel and clicking there hits NOTHING (lands way away from the graph, off the page).
operational_notes.note: OBSERVED 2026-05-19 (Jesse, watching live): worker's click_at coordinates landed 'way away from the graph' — points were not focused. Tab focus then walked through whatever element was actually focused, moving wrong things. The worker MUST derive click_at pixel coords from the SCREENSHOT, not from any AX bbox.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
BEFORE any keyboard nav, the worker MUST use send_to_llm question_type='solve_complex' with the screenshot to extract:
  1) Graph bounding box in screen pixels: top-left (gx0, gy0), bottom-right (gx1, gy1).
  2) Axis tick values: x-axis min/max (xmin, xmax), y-axis min/max (ymin, ymax).
  3) Each currently-visible point's pixel position: for each of the 4 visible blue dots, output (px, py) — even if it's at origin (gx0, gy1).
  4) Pixel-per-unit conversion: ppu_x = (gx1 - gx0) / (xmax - xmin); ppu_y = (gy1 - gy0) / (ymax - ymin).
Then for each Point N at target (tx, ty):
  - Parse AX name to read current (cur_x, cur_y).
  - Skip if cur == target (already placed correctly).
  - click_at(visible_pixel_x, visible_pixel_y) using the LLM-extracted pixel positions — NOT the AXButton bbox.
  - press_key Tab if click_at didn't focus this Point (verify by AXFocused or re-read).
  - Press arrows: number of presses = round((tx-cur_x)/step_x) for x, etc.
  - Step size: do the 1-arrow calibration test ONCE at start (Pass 1 from prior note) to get actual step_x and step_y.
  - wait_for_element 'Point N at <tx> comma <ty>' (3s).
  - On match, move to next Point. On mismatch, calibration might be slightly off — use Shift+Arrow (10x step) to correct.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: DO NOT use AXButton.bbox or AXButton.position for click_at on this widget. The 'Point N at X comma Y' AXButton is a screen-reader-only focusableHandle that lives offscreen. Its bbox is useless for mouse positioning. ALWAYS derive the visible-pixel position from the screenshot via solve_complex first. DO NOT assume points start at origin on retry encounters — read AX names first to get current positions, then compute deltas.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan 'click each dot on the image' interactive — multiple visual targets where each click opens a small selector (more/less/same)
operational_notes.rule: DOTS ARE IN THE IMAGE, NOT AX. The clickable dots (4 in the kinetic-energy / potential-energy 'pick each location' exercises) are rendered as SVG circles or canvas pixels — NOT discrete AX elements. AT-SPI cannot enumerate them. The worker must use solve_complex on the screenshot to extract each dot's PIXEL POSITION + its physics context (above/at/below the reference line, distance change, etc.), then click_at each dot's pixel and select the per-dot answer from the dropdown that opens.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
PHASE 1 DISCOVER (per universal image-discovery rule):
  1a. extract_question — gets the question text + 'Click each dot' instruction.
  1b. send_to_llm question_type=solve_complex with FULL SCREENSHOT, prompt: 'In this Khan Academy image, identify each dot the learner can click. For each dot return: (1) pixel position (x, y) in screenshot coords, (2) physics context — height vs reference line, distance change, charge proximity, etc. (3) the correct answer (more/less/same) for the question being asked. Return JSON: {dots: [{n, px, py, context, answer}, ...]}'.
PHASE 2 SOLVE:
  2a. for_each dot in $plan.dots:
     - click_at(dot.px, dot.py) — opens the per-dot selector dropdown
     - wait_for_element role=AXMenuItem
     - find_and_click target=dot.answer role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact (strategy=mouse_click)
     - wait 0.5s for menu close
  2b. find_and_click target='Check' role=AXButton match_mode=exact post_delay=2.5
PHASE 3 VERIFY: post-Check, if 'Try again' / 'Not quite' appears → wrong-answer-RCA universal rule fires.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT try find_all role=AXButton to find dots — they're not AXButtons. Do NOT use AXImage bbox center as click target — the image contains MULTIPLE dots, the bbox is the whole image. Each dot needs its own pixel position from vision.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan Perseus 'label-image' widget — 'Click each dot on the image' (e.g. Apply: Potential energy ball-trajectory question)
operational_notes.rule: WIDGET = LABEL-IMAGE (not interactive-graph). The dots are REAL HTML <button> elements via Wonder Blocks Clickable, NOT SVG/canvas. find_all role=AXButton SHOULD return them. Yesterday's graph-drag-plot Mafs+@use-gesture analysis does NOT apply — different widget family.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
PROBE FIRST: find_all role=AXButton — filter to buttons inside the question content area (bbox.x > 450, bbox.y in question region) AND with the marker label pattern (often 'Marker 1' / 'Dot 1' / numbered, OR empty names if AX bridge drops aria-label). If NAMES present → AX-name path. If names empty → vision path.

AX-NAME PATH:
  for each dot button: find_and_click target=<dot.name> role=AXButton strategy=mouse_click match_mode=exact → opens SingleSelect → wait_for_element role=AXMenuItem → discover_menu → send_to_llm solve_choice (3 options: more/less/same) with the question + screenshot → find_and_click target=<chosen> role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact → wait 0.5s
  After all dots: find_and_click 'Check' role=AXButton match_mode=exact post_delay=2.5

VISION PATH (if AX names empty):
  PHASE 1 DISCOVER: send_to_llm question_type=solve_complex with screenshot, prompt: 'Identify each dot on the trajectory image. For each return pixel position (x, y) and the correct answer (more/less/same PE relative to shoulder line). JSON: {dots: [{n, px, py, answer}, ...]}'
  PHASE 2 SOLVE: for each dot in plan: click_at(dot.px, dot.py) → wait_for_element AXMenuItem → find_and_click target=dot.answer role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact → wait 0.5s
  PHASE 3: find_and_click 'Check'.

Per-dot selector is Wonder Blocks SingleSelect (same as bare dropdowns) — discover_menu should capture menu items. If discover_menu misses (portalled to document.body), fall back to find_all role=AXMenuItem AFTER the dot-click.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT use find_all role=AXComboBox — the dots are NOT comboboxes, this returns 0. Do NOT assume Mafs / @use-gesture / drag mechanics from yesterday's graph-drag-plot DR — wrong widget family. Do NOT click_at on the WHOLE image bbox center — that's the image container, not a specific dot.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Khan label-image dot-selector — RCA of 2026-05-20 20:32 wrong-answer
operational_notes.rule: PER-DOT VISION CALL REQUIRED for label-image dot-selectors. One large solve_matching call with all 4 dots in one prompt produces wrong answers on subset of dots — LLM context-blends across dots, getting position-vs-reference-line wrong on at least one. The fix is per-dot vision: for each dot, isolate a region screenshot anchored on that dot's pixel position (~150px box around the dot center on the trajectory image), send a per-dot solve_complex with prompt 'Compare ONLY this dot's height to the dotted horizontal reference line through the boy's shoulder. Answer: more / less / the same.' Then aggregate the 4 per-dot answers into the matches dict before select_dropdown_option calls.
operational_notes.observed: 2026-05-20 20:32: BT executed all 4 selections cleanly (menu_closed verify worked thanks to commit b90c436). LLM picked 'the same' for ball-in-right-hand-same-height (correct) and 'less' for ball-rolling-down-hill (correct). One+ of the other 2 dots (above/below reference line) was wrong → Khan rejected. Single-call vision can't reliably reason about 4 independent height comparisons simultaneously.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint:
PER-DOT VISION DISCOVER phase for label-image dot-selectors:
  1. find_all role=AXComboBox (or AXButton if probe shows real button names) → $dots, in DOM order top→bottom left→right.
  2. For each dot popup: read its visible_bbox to compute the dot's pixel position on the trajectory image.
  3. For each dot (one at a time): 
     - send_to_llm question_type=solve_complex with the FULL screenshot AND a per-dot prompt: 'Focus ONLY on dot N at pixel approximately (px, py). Determine its height relative to the dotted horizontal reference line through the boy's shoulder. If above the line → answer "more". If below → "less". If on the line → "the same". Return JSON: {answer: "more|less|same"}'.
     - parse the per-dot answer.
  4. Now per-dot answers are known. Click each $dots[i] to open its SingleSelect, find_and_click target=answer[i] role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact, wait 0.5s.
  5. find_and_click 'Check'.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Do NOT use one solve_matching call for all 4 dots — it produces correct answers on some dots and wrong on others because the LLM splits attention across dots and gets at least one height-vs-reference comparison wrong.

operational_notes.note: BELOW-THE-FOLD DRAG WIDGETS (2026-06-11): the tree CONTAINS off-screen content (names/alt-text readable) but coordinates are only actuation-valid when the widget is ON-SCREEN. Mouse events cannot reach below the fold. TWO-PHASE pattern, fully autonomous with existing primitives: BT#1 = scroll the widget fully into view + wait (END the BT there); the next poll captures the post-scroll tree; BT#2 computes drag coords from THAT capture. NEVER drag with coordinates computed from a pre-scroll tree — they are stale by the scroll delta.

subtypes.name: sorter_orderer
subtypes.description: Items that must be arranged in correct order (orderer) or sorted into categories (sorter/categorizer). Drag-and-drop interface. Perseus widgets: sorter, orderer, categorizer.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: drag items to correct positions or categories -> click 'Check'. May need screenshot + LLM analysis for correct ordering.

operational_notes.screen_variant: Perseus sorter / orderer / EXERCISE_RANKING_DRAG / EXERCISE_SORTABLE_RANKING
operational_notes.rule: DRAG BBOX FROM TREE.JSON + correct nested-dict schema
operational_notes.note: Khan Perseus ranking/sorter widgets present draggable handle elements in the AX tree. find_all does NOT surface visible_bbox in its output, but the full tree.json saved to the consult dir DOES contain visible_bbox: [x, y, width, height] as a 4-element list on every node with positional data (~731 such entries on a typical Khan exercise tree). The worker generating this BT has tree.json available — read drag handle bboxes directly from there, then emit drag actions with correct schema.
operational_notes.drag_handler_schema: drag.params MUST be: {start: {x: <int>, y: <int>}, end: {x: <int>, y: <int>}}. Nested dicts, NOT flat source_x/source_y/target_x/target_y or start_x/start_y. Handler reads start['x'] / start['y'] / end['x'] / end['y'] — anything else returns 'missing/invalid start/end coords' and BT fails.
operational_notes.bt_construction_pattern: 1) Identify draggable handle elements in tree.json (typically AXButton or AXGenericElement nodes containing the rankable items — look at their name/description for the item labels like 'G', 'B', 'R' or full names). 2) Read each handle's visible_bbox = [x, y, w, h]. Center = (x + w/2, y + h/2). 3) send_to_llm with screenshot + question_type=solve_complex; ask for the correct ORDERING (e.g. 'R,B,G' for greatest-to-least). 4) For each item that needs to move: compute source_center (current position) and target_center (the position where it SHOULD be — typically the bbox center of where the target-position handle currently is). 5) Emit one drag action per move with correct nested schema.

params.question: <read screenshot + tree.json bboxes; return ordering as comma-separated letters>

anti_patterns: drag with FLAT keys source_x/source_y/target_x/target_y or start_x/start_y/end_x/end_y — handler rejects, BT fails
Skipping the drag step and only sending to LLM + clicking Check — answer stays in default order, Khan marks wrong
Using fixed coords without consulting tree.json bboxes — coords shift per session/viewport

operational_notes.note: DRAG TIMING (PRODUCTION-PROVEN 2026-06-11: ranking Q answered CORRECT on a live unit test with these exact values): set press_hold: 0.25, release_hold: 0.15 on every drag for this widget. Escalation order if no engagement: press_hold 0.40; then steps: 8 + step_delay: 0.04 (bigger first move). Engagement check: Check button ENABLES on first real widget touch — if Check is still gray after the drag, the widget was never engaged (do not click Check).

DRAG VERIFICATION (Jesse-observed 2026-06-11): mouse drag DOES engage this widget — but AX bboxes go STALE after a DOM reorder, so tree/bbox comparison FALSELY reports 'no movement'. Verify drags by SCREENSHOT (visual order) only. Check-button readiness = AXEnabled attribute, never its bbox. A falsely-failed verifier here caused 3 duplicate drags — always re-derive remaining moves from the CURRENT visual order before dragging again.

IMAGE-PAIR QUESTIONS (2026-06-11, complete-AX live): Khan's AXImage DESCRIPTIONS are the authoritative question content — e.g. 'Two small circles a large distance apart'. subtypes.name: ranking
subtypes.description: Perseus sorter widget operating as a RANKING list (single column, 3+ items to be arranged top-to-bottom by some criterion: mass, size, magnitude, etc.). Distinct from sorter_orderer's category-bucket use case. Variant name suffix: RANKING_DRAG.
subtypes.interaction_pattern: evaluate items by criterion (via screenshot) -> drag handles to reorder if needed -> ALWAYS perform at least one drag to engage widget (Khan disables Check until widget is touched) -> click 'Check'.

operational_notes.note: CLASSIFY: question wording 'rank/order/strongest to weakest/arrange' + a draggable vertical list = EXERCISE_RANKING (NOT bare EXERCISE — bare gets no actuation guidance). NEVER end the BT after send_to_llm without actuation.

operational_notes.by: claude-primary via escalation -> taeys-hands (Family/Perplexity domain knowledge)
operational_notes.screen_variant: Perseus matcher (EXERCISE_MATCHING) — event->energy-change, often animation/image-grounded
operational_notes.rule: Perseus matcher is a DRAG widget, NOT comboboxes and NOT click-to-cycle. find_all(AXComboBox) FAILS here (it matches Chrome tab-strip AXRadioButtons / returns nothing useful) — that was the worker mis-pattern that failed on f05e03e1. Draggable cards are AXCell/AXGroup with EMPTY names: identify them by visible_bbox geometry/order from tree.json, never by name.
operational_notes.note: NEVER a dropdown — clicking cells opens NO AXMenuItems (observed live 2026-06-11: a dropdown-style BT failed at wait_for_element(AXMenuItem), zero menu items). The matcher is a Perseus mouse-event Sortable: actuate by synthesized mouse DRAG from value-bank item center to target cell center; verify after each drop by re-reading the tree. Complete-AX is now PARTIALLY live on the Mac (2026-06-11): AXTable/AXRow/AXCell/AXList structure appears, but on the FIRST capture after Chrome's AX rebuild the value texts can be EMPTY and heights 0 — RECAPTURE the tree (or wait+recapture) before concluding values are unavailable. If texts are still empty after recapture, ground the values from the SCREENSHOT (send_to_llm solve_complex with full screenshot) and drive drags by coordinates: value-bank list x,y and cell x,y ARE present in the tree even when texts are empty. Cards live BELOW THE FOLD frequently -> scroll first. If the question references an animation ('Play Animation'), PLAY it then solve with vision. Do NOT blind-guess a mapping (wrong placement = catastrophic wrong answer).
operational_notes.drag_handler_schema: drag.params MUST be nested: {start:{x:<int>,y:<int>}, end:{x:<int>,y:<int>}}. Read source/target centers from each AXCell visible_bbox=[x,y,w,h] in tree.json (center = [x+w/2, y+h/2]). Same schema as the sorter/ranking note.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: 1) scroll down (amount=5, x2-3 with short waits) until matcher cells are in-tree. 2) if a Play Animation button exists: find_and_click it, wait ~4s. 3) send_to_llm question_type=solve_complex with the full screenshot + the question + event list -> returns event->energy-change pairing. 4) read each event-card and target-slot visible_bbox from tree.json; emit one drag per pair {start:card_center,end:slot_center} (or reorder a single column into the correct sequence). 5) find_and_click Check post_delay=2.5. 6) wrong-answer state -> Cmd+R reset + full-stop RCA, no blind retry.
operational_notes.physics_note_zipliner_example: Worked example (ball-Earth system): pulled up = grav PE increases (energy in); released/swing down = PE->KE; smash wall = KE->thermal+sound. (Per-question physics; the DRAG widget handling above is the generalizable lesson.)

operational_notes.note: drag handler param shape (observed live 2026-06-11, two failed guesses): params MUST be NESTED dicts {"start": {"x": N, "y": N}, "end": {"x": N, "y": N}} plus optional steps/post_delay. Flat keys (start_x/from_x/to_x...) silently return None and fail the BT. Vision solve already produced the correct mapping (solve_matching with screenshot works); only the actuation params were wrong.

operational_notes.rule: CORRECTION to the earlier coordinate-drag note: Perseus matcher is HTML5/React-DnD. SYNTHETIC COORDINATE DRAG (xdotool/CGEvent mousedown->move->mouseup) does NOT actuate it — HTML5 DnD needs real dragstart/DataTransfer events the synthetic pointer path does not emit. That is why every coordinate-drag attempt failed and why send_to_llm 422d (the value labels are not in the AX tree to extract). DO NOT drive this with drag {start,end}.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: Perseus matcher actuation = KEYBOARD DnD via the react-dnd a11y sensor (only reliable synthetic path): per value -> press_key Tab to focus the value-label draggable -> press_key Space (or Enter) to PICK UP (announces grabbed) -> press_key Arrow (Down/Right) to move to the target row -> press_key Space to DROP. Screenshot-verify after each, single attempt, no retry-loop. PREREQUISITE: the source value-bank draggables + cell extents are NOT in the standard tree capture (observed: 10 empty AXCell, top-left x,y only, no w/h, no increases/decreases strings, 3rd row missing). They must be captured first via a FULL app-root re-scan with extents, or OCR of the screenshot to get the value-label centers + map by visual row. Without the draggables captured, keyboard focus order is blind -> do not guess.
operational_notes.note: Physics for the wrecking-ball instance (ball-Earth TOTAL energy): cable raises ball=INCREASES (external work in); swings down (isolated, air-resistance ignored)=STAYS THE SAME (PE<->KE conserved); smashes wall=DECREASES (energy out as thermal/sound/deformation). LIMITATION: this widget is at the edge of current automation — needs Mac-side capture of the DnD value-bank + keyboard-DnD support (Mac code is frozen -> claude-0). Do NOT blind-attempt a drag (wrong placement = catastrophic wrong answer).

operational_notes.by: claude-primary (Grok/LOGOS validation seat of the cycle)
operational_notes.rule: VALIDATION / dissent resolution. The Family SPLIT on the grab key: Gaia (source-verified vs Perseus + react-dnd-accessible-backend README) = Cmd+D; Grok+Gemini+Perplexity (generic a11y assumption, unverified) = Space. DO NOT hardcode either blind — RESOLVE EMPIRICALLY via the aria-live announcer (which only becomes readable AFTER Chrome complete-AX is enabled): focus a draggable, send Cmd+D, read the announcer — if it says "Picked up <X>" the grab worked (react-dnd, Cmd+D); if silent, send Space (the @dnd-kit/dnd-kit-KeyboardSensor path). Whichever produces the Picked-up announcement is correct for this widget. Arrows then Enter/Space to drop; re-read announcer/cell to confirm before the next pair.
operational_notes.note: CAPTURE fallback (Grok, if complete-AX truly cannot be enabled Mac-side): OCR the matcher bounding box (macOS Vision) to map value labels + drop-zone labels to x,y by row centroid, then drive the keyboard sequence + click_at for focus entry. complete-AX (Gaia) is preferred — it fixes geometry AND exposes the announcer (the verification channel); OCR gives positions but not the announcer, so verification is weaker. Consensus across all sources: coordinate CGEvent drag NEVER actuates (HTML5 DnD trusted-pointer gate); synthetic KEY events always do.

subtypes.name: label_image
subtypes.description: Image with hotspots or blank labels to fill in. User clicks dots/regions and selects or types labels. Perseus widget: label-image.

operational_notes.note: Wonder Blocks renders dropdowns as AXComboBox at depth ~32. Each combobox's value attribute carries the dot description (e.g. 'a sphere with a negative sign located outside the center of the atom'). find_and_click can match these by value substring + role=AXComboBox + match_mode=contains. After click, the dropdown options render as AXMenuItem at SHALLOW depth (~14, OUTSIDE the AXWebArea). handle_find_and_click for AXMenuItem first searches web-area scope (returns nothing for these popups), then falls through to find_element which walks full app and finds them. Menu item values are LOWERCASE ('electron', 'proton', 'neutron') — must use lowercase target text or matching fails.
operational_notes.bt_template_hint: find_and_click target=<combobox value substring> role=AXComboBox match_mode=contains -> wait 1s -> find_and_click target=<lowercase-answer> role=AXMenuItem match_mode=exact
